chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI SCRA 797 of 2023 ____________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ____________________________________________________________ 1. For order on office objection No.20 2. For hearing of CMA No.775/2023 3. For hearing of main case 4. For hearing of CMA No.776/2023 20.04.2026 Mr. Faheem Raza khuhro, advocate for the applicant Learned counsel had proposed following question of law for determination: Whether the Customs Appellate Tribunal has erred in law by releasing Indian origin wrist watches which release / redemption against fine is prohibited under Paragraph 5 of the Import Policy Order 2022 amended vide SRO-927(I)/2019 dated 09.08.2019 stipulating ban on import of goods of Indian or Israeli origin or goods imported from India or Israel? Notwithstanding the foregoing, learned counsel states that the impugned judgment is devoid of any independent discussion and / or deliberation and merely rested on reproduction. Counsel for the applicant places courier tracking report on record which demonstrates that service has been effected upon the respondent. The Appellate Tribunal is the last fact-finding forum in the statutory hierarchy; therefore, it is incumbent upon it to render independent deliberations and findings on each issue. The manner in which the appeals in general are to be addressed has been emphasized by the Supreme Court in the judgment reported as 2019 SCMR 1726. This High Court has consistently maintained that the Appellate Tribunal is required to proffer independent reasons and findings, and in the absence thereof a perfunctory order could not be sustained.

The manner in which the appeals in general are to be addressed has been emphasized by the Supreme Court in the judgment reported as 2019 SCMR 1726. This High Court has consistently maintained that the Appellate Tribunal is required to proffer independent reasons and findings, and in the absence thereof a perfunctory order could not be sustained. Reliance is placed on the judgment dated 02.10.2024 in SCRA 1113 of 2023 and judgment dated 27.08.2024 in SCRA 757 of 2015. Earlier Division Bench judgments have also maintained that if the impugned order is discrepant in the manner as aforesaid, the correct course is to remand the matter for adjudication afresh. Reliance is placed on the judgment dated 10.12.2024 in ITRA 343 of 2024. We are of the considered view that the impugned judgment could not be considered to be a speaking order and is prima facie devoid of any independent reasoning etc. The entire judgment comprises essentially of reproduction and is crowned with a dissonant conclusion. Hence, no case is set forth to sustain the impugned judgment, which is hereby set aside and the matter is remanded back to the Appellate Tribunal for adjudication afresh in accordance with law. A copy of this decision may also be sent under the seal of this Court and signature of the Registrar to the learned Customs Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 196 of the Customs Act, 1969. Judge Judge Amjad
